description_long: This is a rectangular, three-story office building with a flat, dark roof and a tan brick exterior. The front facade features large, vertically aligned windows and a prominent ground-level entrance, typical of early 20th-century commercial architecture. The side walls are mostly unadorned, with minimal window openings and a visible external chimney structure. The roof includes HVAC equipment and utility fixtures, suggesting modern retrofitting. The rear of the building has fewer windows and a secondary entrance, likely for service or staff access. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with subtle decorative elements above the windows and at the roofline.
description: A three-story tan brick office building with a flat roof, large front windows, and minimal side detailing. The structure features a prominent main entrance and rooftop utility fixtures.
description_view_0: Side view showing the plain tan brick wall, sloped roof edge, and a tall external chimney.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance, large vertically aligned windows, and subtle decorative trim.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a similar plain brick wall, visible rooftop equipment, and no major openings.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a secondary entrance, fewer windows, and the back of the chimney structure.
attribute_window_pattern: Regularly spaced, vertically aligned rectangular windows on the front facade; minimal and irregular windows on the sides and rear.
